title: Files Available on Public Shared View
excerpt:
For client facing views, it would be AMAZING and simple to make files viewable on the public link. I’m a little perplexed as why they’re not available for view on the public view. 
There’s a thread where many have reques…

posts.author: davidpriceav (David Price)
posts.cookedHtml:
For client facing views, it would be AMAZING and simple to make files viewable on the public link. I’m a little perplexed as why they’re not available for view on the public view.

There’s a thread where many have requested this, but I noticed it wasn’t in the “Submit an Idea” area.

posts.author: sfeagans (Shawn Feagans)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be really helpful for us also, for people who don’t need full Monday licenses but just need to be able to review a board and download related files.

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @davidpriceav and @sfeagans

better late than never. You might want to try out our most recent app Board to Website Widget for that.

Turn your boards into shareable website widgets that include all the board data and files that are attached to it. No need to fiddle around with board permissions, inviting guests or adding extra seats.

Check out the demos for some inspiration, and let me know what you think.

– Markus

posts.author: lewissmithsailing (Lewis Smith)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MASO, I’ve just installed the widget and it works great! However, I was wondering if there is any functionality or piece of code I can embed alongside the iFrame HTML that removes the internal iFrame scrolling?

  • Lewis

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lewissmithsailing

Great to read that you like the app so far. If you use the Embed code provided in the app’s publish modal, there should be a JavaScript included by default that automatically resizes the iframe to get rid of any outer scrollbars.

Do you mind to share the link where you embedded the widget via private message or by using our contact form Contact · Gorilla Apps?

So I can have a look at it.

– Markus

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any development in this regard?

posts.author: davidpriceav (David Price)
posts.cookedHtml: No. There is still not way to do this in a table without adding the client as a user.
